label: Lamplens Asteroid
description: Lamplens Asteroid occupies a stable arc within Vesperglass System’s vitrified debris belt, its exterior fused into glassy planes that hold the star’s copper-white attenuation as hard reflections. A persistent electrostatic charge runs along fracture seams, organizing drifting dust into thin, luminous filaments that map the surrounding radiance currents. Impact terraces and melt-scar basins give the body a faceted, architectural profile, as if cut to serve the system’s orderly geometry. Navigation charts mark it as a reliable visual reference against the mirror-halo’s long twilight gradients.
